name: Identity
description: Credential issuance and selective disclosure starbridge-app (dregg-native).

deos.mount: register_deos (src/lib.rs) — folds the composed DeosApp into the StarbridgeAppContext affordance registry; DeosApp::mount yields the axum router
deos.publish: Signature (the issuer cell is a dregg:// sturdyref a relying party — a verifier on another federation — reacquires to verify credentials across the trust boundary)

affordances.verify: cap-only, Signature (a VERIFIER reads + re-derives a presentation)
affordances.present: cap-only, Either (a PRESENTER produces a disclosure; a holder-side read path)
affordances.issue: GATED (cap∧state), None/root — schema-bound precondition (SCHEMA_COMMITMENT>=1); the fire advances ISSUANCE_COUNTER by exactly +1 off live state, the executor re-enforcing MonotonicSequence(ISSUANCE_COUNTER)
affordances.revoke: GATED (cap∧state), None/root — something-issued precondition (ISSUANCE_COUNTER>=1); the fire advances REVOCATION_ROOT (strictly greater), the executor re-enforcing Monotonic(REVOCATION_ROOT)

deos.seam: CLOSED — INCLUDING THE AUTHORITY TOOTH. The issue/revoke fires run against the seeded issuer cell carrying the FULL floor issuer_program (WriteOnce(SCHEMA_COMMITMENT) + MonotonicSequence(ISSUANCE_COUNTER) + Monotonic(REVOCATION_ROOT) + SenderAuthorized(PublicRoot(ISSUER_AUTH_ROOT_SLOT))), all four RE-ENFORCED by the executor on the submission path: a non-+1 issuance and a revocation-root rewind BITE, and the now-REAL MerkleMembership STARK verifier (the EmbeddedExecutor's embedded runtime defaults to registry_with_real_verifiers) enforces SenderAuthorized for real. seed_issuer seeds ISSUER_AUTH_ROOT = single_member_authorized_root(signer_pk) and the fires attach single_member_membership_proof(signer_pk) as a MerklePath witness, so the honest issuer's issue/revoke fire GREEN through the real authority tooth; a non-member signer (or the right signer with no/wrong proof) is REFUSED by the real STARK. tests/deos_seam.rs demonstrates both faces: (b) the authorized signer issues green through the real verifier carrying the proof (and (b') the same signer with no proof is refused — the proof does the work); (d) a non-member signer is refused by the real MerkleMembership STARK. (tests/factory_birth.rs — a floor test — still asserts the fail-closed red path on factory-born cells via unwitnessed hostile turns, which the real verifier refuses too.)
